claim_text: SECOND ME parameterises a user's memory into L2 model weights, layered above L0 (raw data) and L1 (natural-language memory).
notes: Three-layer model is the central contribution; consistently described across Abstract, §2.1, §2.2.

claim_text: SECOND ME serves as a 'context provider' rather than a task executor, aligning with the user's perspective.
evidence_location: §2.2 — 'we position SECOND ME as a context provider aligned with the user's perspective, rather than a task executor.'
notes: Direct author statement; positioned against general-purpose LLM agents.

claim_text: The training pipeline is fully automated and comprises data synthesis, five-level filtering, PEFT SFT, and DPO.
The base model is Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and training starts with PEFT.
evidence_location: §3.1 — 'Our base model, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Qwen et al., 2025), undergoes automatic training and evaluation.'
notes: Specific model name + size in body text.

claim_text: Three answer-style COT strategies are used during training: Weak, Multi-step, and Strong (the last using Deepseek-R1 with strict format constraints).
notes: Each strategy is described with concrete mechanics and length constraints.

claim_text: DPO does not introduce new knowledge but refines preference alignment on approximately 20% of the SFT data.
evidence_location: §3.3 — 'Preference pairs constitute approximately 20% of the total SFT training data.'
notes: Quantitative proportion is in the body.

claim_text: Evaluation uses four metrics: Memory (Self), Memory (Third-party), Context Enhance, and Context Critic.
Strong COT significantly improves Memory (Self) and Context Critic performance; Multi-step COT often underperforms Weak COT.
evidence_location: Table 1 (extracted): Strong Memory (Self) = 0.91 vs Weak = 0.86 vs Multi-step = 0.64.
notes: Table 1 numerical values reproduced from extracted text.

claim_text: DPO brings substantial improvements across all tasks when combined with iterative COT refinement.
evidence_location: §3.5 — 'Table 2 demonstrates that DPO brings substantial improvements'.
notes: Table 2 layout was not cleanly extracted (pdftotext -layout limitation); specific numbers in Table 2 not recoverable from extraction.

claim_text: Human evaluation reports Strong-COT-with-DPO scores close to 1 and Strong-COT-without-DPO at ~0.95 average.
evidence_location: §3.5 — 'Human evaluation shows Strong COT without DPO achieves an average score of 0.95, while Strong COT with DPO scores close to 1.'
notes: Numbers are author-reported; independent human eval not in the paper.

claim_text: SECOND ME drives a 3 to 5 orders of magnitude network-efficiency gain via Metcalfe's Law in human-AI networks.
evidence_location: §4 — 'integration of human and AI nodes increases network efficiency by 3 to 5 orders of magnitude.'
notes: This is a Metcalfe-style projection, not a controlled measurement. Independent verification absent in the paper.

claim_text: The full system is open-sourced at github.com/Mindverse/Second-Me (note: Mindverse, not mindhive).
notes: URL is in the abstract and at the end of §4; org name is Mindverse.
